briefing_memo: GC Strategies was awarded ArriveCan contracts worth at least $59 million according to the Auditor General, with over $19 million directly documented in procurement records. Public Services and Procurement Canada banned the company from all government contracts for seven years effective June 6, 2025 (nine months ago), following an assessment that deemed the supplier's conduct ineligible; concurrently, a Globe and Mail investigation alleged collusion with CBSA officials and falsification of subcontractor credentials (October 2023, globalnews.ca and search.open.canada.ca). No remediation signals—such as leadership changes, integrity certifications, or settlements—appear in available records, and the seven-year debarment remains an active sanction disqualifying the entity from federal procurement. Given the current debarment status and absence of documented remediation, no federal funding should be extended to GC Strategies unless and until the supplier regains eligibility through formal reinstatement by Public Services and Procurement Canada.

actions.title: Enforce existing 7-year government contract suspension
actions.rationale: Entity is currently barred from all government contracts following ArriveCan procurement irregularities, as confirmed by multiple June 2025 sources.

actions.title: Verify suspension is registered in all procurement systems
actions.rationale: Confirm that the entity's debarment status is reflected in SAP, GETS, and all departmental vendor databases to prevent inadvertent re-engagement.
evidence: Recent AG findings on procurement controls

actions.title: Review all historical contracts for recovery opportunities
actions.rationale: Auditor General report characterized ArriveCan contracting as having irregularities; assess whether prior payments warrant clawback or legal recovery action.
evidence: Multiple CRITICAL news on procurement irregularities
